description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical shape, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each showing natural wood grain, weathering, and some dark stains, giving it a rustic, aged appearance. The barrel is reinforced by four prominent metal hoops, two near the top and two near the bottom, each fastened with evenly spaced rivets. The top is open, revealing a dark, empty interior, and there are no visible handles or lids. The wood appears to be untreated or only lightly finished, and the metal bands have a slightly worn, matte finish. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage areas, or near workshops, often used for storage or as a decorative element.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops and an open top, showing natural wood grain and signs of age. The barrel is reinforced by four metal bands and lacks handles or a lid.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open top, wood grain, and all four metal hoops clearly, with some dark stains on the wood.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the curvature of the barrel and the even spacing of the metal hoops, with subtle wood texture variations.
description_view_2: Another side view, nearly identical to view 1, showing the same metal banding and wood grain, with minor differences in wood stain patterns.
description_view_3: This rear view displays the same features as the front, with the open top and visible metal hoops, but with slightly different wood grain and stain marks.
